remote_base.role: pulled merge base and persisted push provenance

local_edited.role: local plan source after user edits

remote_changed.role: the same remote site after independent live drift between dry-run and apply

runner.role: the only process allowed to compare, upload, inspect, and recover

shape: remote-base pulls first and seeds the merge base
local-edited is restored from that base and edited independently
runner calls push_preflight, push_snapshot_hashes, push_plan_dry_run, push_batch_apply, push_journal, and push_recover against the same remote site as it drifts
push_plan_dry_run is only an eligibility receipt; push_batch_apply must revalidate live remote state before every batch and again at the storage boundary
push_journal and push_recover inspect are read-only evidence readers, while mutating recovery modes require fresh live proof
remote-db and local-db remain isolated so live drift does not contaminate the local edit history
use only the sandbox-provided 8080 ingress through a local-only proxy when browser-visible inspection is needed
remote-base seeds the pull base and push identity evidence
local-edited is the imported site after local changes
remote-changed is the same remote site after it drifts independently and forces stale-apply rejection and recovery
the runner proves dry-run and apply are separate by taking a fresh snapshot listing, uploading a dry-run plan, then applying only after live revalidation succeeds
the runner keeps the three blueprints isolated and does not open a public tunnel
use only the sandbox-provided 8080 ingress through a local-only proxy when browser-visible inspection is needed
